description_long: This is a light gray kepi-style hat, commonly associated with 19th-century military uniforms, particularly those of the American Civil War. The hat features a cylindrical, slightly tapered crown with a flat top and a short, flat, black visor at the front. A black band encircles the base of the crown, secured at the front with a small, square, gold-colored buckle and round gold buttons on each side. The fabric appears to be a textured, woven material, likely wool or cotton, with a matte finish. The interior is not visible, but it is likely lined with a softer fabric for comfort. The overall design is utilitarian with minimal decorative elements, emphasizing function over ornamentation.
description: A light gray kepi-style hat with a flat top, black visor, and a black band with gold accents, reminiscent of 19th-century military headwear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flat-topped crown, black visor, and the black band with a gold buckle and side buttons.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the short, flat black visor and the gold button securing the black band.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain, uninterrupted light gray crown with no visible visor or band details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the black visor and gold button on the other side.
